titleStmt.title: Letter from William Pearse Young and James Meeke, 2 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James Meeke

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by two Presbyterian clergymen, William Young Pearse (b. 1883) and James Meeke (b. 1843). The letter concerns the candidature of Rev. James Moody to the appointment of chaplain of Presbyterian inmates at Dundalk prison.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/1623/2

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
The Presbyterian Church in Ireland. NEWRY PRESBYTERY,
REV. JAMES MEEKE, B.A.,
CLERK Seaview,
Warrenpoint,.....191... Downshire Road, Newry. 2nd May, 1916. The Newry Presbytery being met and constituted, -
inter alia. The Rev. James Moody, The Mause,, Dundalk, was
nominated for the position of Chaplain to the Presbyterian
inmates in Dundalk prison in succession to
the late Rev. S. L. Harrison, Castelbellingham. Signed William Pearse Young,
Moderator. James Meeke
clerk.
